mission.title: Install and Configure Keylime on Kubernetes
mission.description: Keylime is an open-source scalable trust system that leverages TPM technology to bootstrap and maintain trust on edge, cloud, and IoT devices. Installing Keylime allows you to ensure the integrity of remote machines and securely manage cryptographic trust.

steps.title: Add the Keylime Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Keylime Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add keylime https://keylime.dev/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Keylime using Helm
steps.description:
Install Keylime with the specified version and create a namespace for it:
```bash
helm install keylime keylime/keylime --version 7.14.1 --namespace keylime --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Keylime components are running correctly:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace keylime
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Keylime components by editing the Helm release:
```bash
helm upgrade keylime keylime/keylime --namespace keylime --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=512Mi
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for Keylime
steps.description:
Create a Role and RoleBinding to allow Keylime to access necessary resources:
```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  namespace: keylime
  name: keylime-role
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["pods", "pods/log"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
EOF

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: keylime-role-binding
  namespace: keylime
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: keylime-role
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: default
  namespace: keylime
EOF
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Keylime will show all Keylime pods running in the 'keylime' namespace. You will also have configured resource limits and RBAC for secure operation.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add keylime https://keylime.dev/charts
helm repo update
helm upgrade keylime keylime/keylime --namespace keylime --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=512Mi

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  namespace: keylime
  name: keylime-role
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["pods", "pods/log"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
EOF

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: keylime-role-binding
  namespace: keylime
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: keylime-role
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: default
  namespace: keylime
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed on your local machine.
